item.title: Version 2.9.0
item.description:
# StatsBar v2.9.0
Even less background work, plus a batch of robustness fixes.
- **iPhone/iPad:** the menu-bar battery glyph now uses a lightweight read, while the heavier diagnostics read (temperature and detailed battery) runs only while the popover is open — so fewer helper processes fire when a phone is connected but you're not looking at it. Hot-battery alerts stay as responsive as before. - **Battery:** macOS's "Maximum Capacity" health figure is fetched only while the battery popover is open (it's a detail-only number), so the periodic `system_profiler` call no longer runs in the background. - **Updates:** information-only Sparkle updates now open their info page instead of offering a broken download, and a mid-download content-length change no longer skews the progress bar. - **Under the hood:** background-read timing is now on a monotonic clock (immune to system clock changes), the network-interface MAC read avoids undefined pointer access, the DNS server list renders stably when addresses repeat, and the public-IP lookup tidies up its per-fetch network session.
_Not notarized — right-click → Open on first launch (or `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/StatsBar.app`)._

item.title: Version 2.8.0
item.description:
# StatsBar v2.8.0
Less work when you're not looking.
- **Menu-bar toggles now cut real work:** turning a metric off (Battery / CPU / RAM / Network) actually stops its background reader instead of just hiding the icon — so hiding what you don't use lowers CPU and energy. It resumes the instant you turn it back on or open the popover. - **Lower idle usage:** each menu-bar icon is redrawn only when its value changes (and never while hidden); the battery reads back off to a lazy poll — refreshed immediately on plug/unplug via a power-source notification — and the network reader stops entirely when its rate isn't on screen. - **iPhone/iPad:** far fewer background helper processes when a phone is connected but nothing is showing it, and the device's name/model are cached instead of re-read every second. - **Under the hood:** the battery and CPU sensor readers now share a single SMC connection.
_Not notarized — right-click → Open on first launch (or `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/StatsBar.app`)._

item.title: Version 2.7.0
item.description:
# StatsBar v2.7.0
More performance and reach.
- **Network:** latency and reachability now work on IPv6-only connections. Previously the ping was IPv4-only, so an IPv6-only link showed the internet as unreachable with no latency even when it was fine. - **Lower idle usage:** iPhone/iPad and Android battery polling now backs off to a slow keep-warm when neither the popover nor the phone menu-bar item is showing — no more shelling out to libimobiledevice/adb every second for a phone nothing is watching. (Hot-battery alerts for a connected iPhone stay as responsive as before.) - **Under the hood:** the readers' background-read throttling is unified into one helper, and the SMC sensor reads skip a redundant syscall per key.
_Not notarized — right-click → Open on first launch (or `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/StatsBar.app`)._

item.title: Version 2.6.0
item.description:
# StatsBar v2.6.0
Performance and polish.
- **Android (multi-device):** fixed battery health being dropped or misreported when more than one Android phone is connected — each device's state is now tracked independently. - **CPU:** the frequency readout no longer shows a wrong value for one tick right after reopening the popover. - **Lower idle usage:** battery power-rail/fan SMC reads and iPhone/iPad USB polling now back off while their panel is closed, cutting background work. - **UI:** the Android "show more" expand no longer stutters; removed a duplicate "Status" row in the Network panel.
_Not notarized — right-click → Open on first launch (or `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/StatsBar.app`)._
